string.text: Global settings that affect how all archetypes are assigned and displayed. Use the tier sub-tabs to manage individual archetypes.

How NPCs receive their archetypes. Hybrid (recommended): assigns on spawn, with fallback recovery when you talk to an NPC. Strict Spawn-Only: only on spawn - if missed, that NPC stays generic. Online-Only: assigns when you enter an NPC's area.
Show a brief PDA message when an NPC receives an archetype assignment. Useful to see the system working; disable for a cleaner HUD.
Show PDA news when an archetyped NPC dies or you enter an area where one operates. Each archetype defines its own news text in its creation pack.
Remove vanilla dialog options (trade, repair, guide, etc.) from archetyped NPCs based on their creation pack settings. Disable to keep all vanilla dialogs alongside ARCH ones.

Removes archetype assignments from ALL NPCs. IMPORTANT: NPC LOADOUT (weapons, armor) WILL NOT CHANGE -- items already in their inventory stay. Names and trade presets reset after SAVE AND RELOAD. For a clean slate, clear assignments then save and reload.

Click to remove all archetype assignments from every NPC in the game. Story NPCs (medics, techs, barmen) are preserved. NPC LOADOUT WILL STAY THE SAME -- spawned items cannot be removed. Save and reload for names and trade presets to fully reset.

Removes archetype assignments from story NPCs only (medics, techs, barmen targeted via assign_to). Use this if a story quest is broken due to an ARCH assignment. Regular NPC assignments are not affected.

Click to remove archetype assignments from story NPCs (vanilla medics, techs, barmen). Their native dialogs will be fully restored after save and reload. They will be re-assigned on next encounter unless the Story NPC pack is disabled.

Clears all assignments and immediately re-rolls archetypes for every online NPC. IMPORTANT: For reassignment to fully take effect, SAVE AND RELOAD after clicking. NPC LOADOUT WILL STAY THE SAME -- spawned weapons and armor cannot be removed from NPCs.

Click to clear and immediately reassign archetypes to all online NPCs. Offline NPCs get assigned on next spawn. SAVE AND RELOAD for names and trade to fully update. NPC LOADOUT (weapons, armor) WILL NOT CHANGE for currently alive NPCs.
Live assignment statistics. Shows how many NPCs have archetypes, broken down by tier, faction, and location. Refreshes each time you open MCM.

Enable or disable individual archetypes and adjust their selection chance. Use the Clear button to remove assignments for a specific archetype. Changes to chance take effect on next assignment (save and reload recommended).
No archetypes registered at this tier. Load a save with creation packs enabled to see archetypes here.
Enable or disable this archetype. Disabled archetypes will not be assigned to any NPC.
Selection weight when multiple archetypes compete for the same NPC. Higher = more likely to be picked. 0 = never assigned.
Remove this archetype from all NPCs that currently have it. They will be eligible for reassignment on next encounter.
Settings for the ARCH task system (fetch, delivery, talk, collect) - map markers and completion alerts.
Show a PDA map marker on the task giver when you have all the required items and the task is ready to turn in.
Show a one-time PDA notification when a fetch task becomes ready to complete (all items collected).

Limit how many archetypes of each tier can be active per map level and per faction. Tier 1 is the highest priority, Tier 5 is the lowest. Set 0 for unlimited. Lower caps mean more variety; higher caps let more NPCs share the same archetype tier.
Maximum number of Tier 1 archetypes that can be assigned on a single map level. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 1 archetypes that can be assigned within a single faction. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 2 archetypes that can be assigned on a single map level. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 2 archetypes that can be assigned within a single faction. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 3 archetypes that can be assigned on a single map level. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 3 archetypes that can be assigned within a single faction. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 4 archetypes that can be assigned on a single map level. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 4 archetypes that can be assigned within a single faction. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 5 archetypes that can be assigned on a single map level. 0 = unlimited.
Maximum number of Tier 5 archetypes that can be assigned within a single faction. 0 = unlimited.
Developer and diagnostic tools. Most players can leave these at defaults.
Show a small on-screen popup with the NPC's archetype ID when you interact with them. Helpful for verifying assignments.
Write ARCH runtime log messages to the game log file. Disable to reduce log noise if everything is working.
Log detailed data every time you interact with an NPC - archetype state, dialog data, trade profile. Generates significant log output.
Track internal performance statistics (assignment counts, timing, cache hits). Use with the console command debug_helper.print_perf_stats() to view results.
Run a consistency check on all loaded archetypes when the game starts. Reports problems to the log. Adds a brief delay on first load.
